clean_body: You don’t know me, but you know what men like me stand for and our reputation | Peter, My name is Adam Schwarze, and I’m a former Navy SEAL, Marine Corps veteran, and proud Minnesotan running for U.S. Senate against Tim Walz’s handpicked lieutenant governor—a politician even more radical than her boss. I’m requesting your support in this critical battle to restore common-sense conservative values to Minnesota. I hold a Master’s from Harvard, a Master’s Certificate from Johns Hopkins in Security and Nuclear Deterrence, and an MBA. But my proudest achievement is serving our country to make my parents, especially my mom, proud. I want to serve you in the U.S. Senate, fighting for the values that have made Minnesota and America great for generations. I’m starting this race with absolutely nothing , but because of the enormity of the race's outcome, I figured there is no harm in asking for your help. At the end of the day, my request of you will end in one of two ways: 1.) . 2.) you read this email without contributing, but you at least now know who I am. I’m not a career politician. I’m a regular guy from a working-class family who grew up playing hockey and answered our nation’s call on my 18th birthday in October 2001, just weeks after the 9/11 attacks. I joined the Marine Corps to defend our country, and as a Navy SEAL, I deployed nine times to over 70 countries, risking my life to protect our freedoms and way of life. Unlike Tim Walz, who retired from the National Guard before his unit deployed to Iraq, I never backed down from serving our nation during war. Minnesota is at a crossroads. Under Governor Walz and his lieutenant governor, our state has been dragged left by radical policies that fueled the violent unrest in Minneapolis in 2020, defunded our police , and burdened hardworking families with higher taxes and inflation. While our streets burned, the lieutenant governor pushed lawsuits against law enforcement, undermining the officers keeping us safe. This isn’t the Minnesota I grew up in—or the one any Minnesotan deserves. If you are reading this email right now and you don’t live in Minnesota, then I’m sure you’ll agree with me that every American must be represented by individuals in Congress who want to build a better version of the America we live in today—individuals of integrity. That’s not who my opponent is. We have an incredible opportunity to elect a Republican to the U.S. Senate next year - something voters in this state haven’t done since they re-elected former Senator Norm Coleman in 2002. As a Republican, I know the odds are tough in a state that hasn’t voted red in a presidential election since 1976. But as a former Navy SEAL, I thrive in tough fights. I’ve faced worse odds overseas and am ready to bring that same grit to Washington. Here’s what I stand for: Restoring law and order by supporting our police and cracking down on crime. Cutting taxes and regulations to ease the burden on retirees and small businesses. Securing our borders to protect our communities and sovereignty. Defending our Second Amendment rights and traditional values. Stopping the radical left’s agenda that’s eroding the America we love. You don’t know me, but you know what men like me stand for and our reputation. So, . This race won’t be easy. The Democrats have deep pockets, and the radical left will stop at nothing to keep this seat. I’m running to serve you, to protect our way of life, and to ensure Minnesota remains a place where faith, family, and freedom thrive. I hope you’ll invest in me right now – any amount will help. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA** | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate

name: Mary Trump
subject: this fight is personal for me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
| Friends, I never needed political pundits or psychologists to tell me that my uncle Donald shouldn’t be anywhere near the Oval Office. I spent much of my childhood in the very house where Donald grew up. The cruel, weak, and deeply insecure man who brought our democracy to the brink of disaster is the same man I have always known. _Seeing him sworn in for a second time was an absolute gut punch._ This fight is personal for me, and as more people lose their jobs or have their rights and freedoms trampled, it’s becoming personal for them, too. **I’m committed to fighting back, but I’m under no illusion about the uphill battle we face. We need everyone to speak up.[Will you split a $25 donation between my org Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton to push back against my uncle and his dangerous, hateful agenda?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112068926/559315480/-204460493?refcode=250723_MaryTrump_DDF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmI0MjgwMC1jYjY4LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m1HbGiFaF4NCjrF4WhbygLUZ904WOrNsc-gyVRvHmVA=)** I launched the Democracy Defense Fund to build a grassroots movement to secure our electoral system from Donald and the authoritarian traitors who seek to destroy it. In order to finally defeat Donald once and for all, we must:
1. _**Flip the House.** The GOP’s majority is historically small and ripe for the taking, and if we retake the House, we can put a critical check on Donald’s power._

2. _**Take Back the Senate.** We need a Democratic majority in the Senate to fight back against judicial appointments and prevent Donald from securing a generation of MAGA rule._

3. _**Take the fight to the States.** We must continue to win critical gubernatorial elections and invest in flippable down-ballot races that have the potential to push back on Donald’s dangerous agenda through state legislatures._

4. _**Win in 2025.** Virginia, New Jersey, Louisiana, and Mississippi all have important legislative elections in 2025, and both New Jersey and Virginia will also hold gubernatorial elections._

**Winning big in these races would shift the narrative of GOP dominance and give Democrats serious momentum heading into a pivotal 2026 election cycle.** [**Can I count on you to help me build and grow the grassroots network we need to fight back against Donald, Elon Musk, and the GOP? If so, please split $25 between my org Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton today!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112068927/559315481/-204460493?refcode=250723_MaryTrump_DDF&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NmI0MjgwMC1jYjY4LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=m1HbGiFaF4NCjrF4WhbygLUZ904WOrNsc-gyVRvHmVA=) In solidarity, Dr. Mary L. Trump

Back-to-School

Comes With a Hidden Agenda

GoldCare and Moms for America expose what's really being pushed!

What if back-to-school vaccine mandates have nothing to do with keeping kids healthy?

GoldCare and
Moms for America

are exposing what’s really being pushed this school year.

At this Townhall, you’ll see real pediatric data comparing vaxxed and unvaxxed children, uncover how school policies are
undermining parental authority, learn how to secure exemptions with confidence, and discover how GoldCare and Moms for America
support families who choose conviction over compliance.

Featuring:

* Kimberly Fletcher (Moms for America) – Founder of a 500,000-strong movement defending faith, family, and parental rights.

* Dr. Paul Thomas – One of America’s most respected pediatricians, with 20+ years exposing the truth about vaccines.

* Dr. Simone Gold (GoldCare) – Physician, attorney, and champion for medical freedom.

The mandates are coming. Don’t wait.

[] [] [] Back-to-School Comes With a Hidden Agenda GoldCare and Moms for America expose what's really being pushed! What if back-to-school vaccine mandates have nothing to do with keeping kids healthy? GoldCare and Moms for America are exposing what’s really being pushed this school year. At this Townhall, you’ll see real pediatric data comparing vaxxed and unvaxxed children, uncover how school policies are undermining parental authority, learn how to secure exemptions with confidence, and discover how GoldCare and Moms for America support families who choose conviction over compliance. Featuring: Kimberly Fletcher (Moms for America) – Founder of a 500,000-strong movement defending faith, family, and parental rights. Dr. Paul Thomas – One of America’s most respected pediatricians, with 20+ years exposing the truth about vaccines. Dr. Simone Gold (GoldCare) – Physician, attorney, and champion for medical freedom. The mandates are coming. name: Adam Schiff
subject: Every American deserves to know this
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/62?t=1001&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]Adam Schiff for Senate

Derek —

I’m not going to sugarcoat it: We’re up against a movement that’s
determined to tear down our democracy and a man in the White House who’ll
do anything to expand his power.

We’ve got to keep calling out the danger — that’s why I’m reaching out
today to ask you to [ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=1004&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]chip in before our end-of-month fundraising
deadline to help me keep fighting back against Trump’s attacks on the rule
of law.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2907?c0=q32025_abs_em.w4l6oI&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1005&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]DONATE

Recently, on the Senate floor, I laid out ten ways Donald Trump is
wrecking this country. Every American should know that Trump is failing us
by:

10. Undermining American innovation: Trump is attacking our world-class
research institutions and shutting out international talent, limiting our
leadership in science, technology, and medicine.

9. Slashing food assistance: He’s gutting the supplemental nutrition
programs that keep millions of American families healthy and ensure our
kids don’t go to bed hungry.

8. Abandoning our allies: Trump is weakening our international
partnerships, cozying up with dictators, and making America less safe.

7. Sabotaging renewable energy: He’s killing clean energy projects, ceding
global renewable energy leadership to China, raising utility bills, and
making us more dependent on fossil fuels.

6. Damaging public health: He’s slashing funding for the National
Institutes of Health (NIH) and key research programs, meaning cures will
be delayed, clinical trials will end, and life-saving care will be harder
to access.

5. Crippling essential services with DOGE cuts: From Social Security to
wildfire response to public education, Trump is making major funding cuts
without worrying about the impact on families, seniors, and kids across
the country who will be hurt.

4. Tanking the economy with tariffs: His trade wars are driving up the
costs of everyday goods like clothes and groceries while alienating our
allies.

3. Stripping away healthcare: Under Trump’s new budget, millions of
Americans will lose healthcare coverage. Many rural clinics and hospitals
will be forced to close, and all of our healthcare costs will go up.

2. Undermining the rule of law: Trump has been treating the Department of
Justice (DOJ) as his own personal political weapon, using it to shield his
allies and target his enemies.

1. Threatening the very idea of America: He’s eroding the values that
built this country: self-governance, equality, and the belief that no one
is above the law — not even the president.

I know this is a lot. And overcoming it will be hard, especially in the
near term when they control everything. But we will take back the
government and put an end to this madness.

The GOP is trying to flood the zone so that we’re too overwhelmed to act.
But I also know that we’re not powerless, and that we can come together,
organize, mobilize, and take our country back from Trump.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&t=1006&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]If you’re with me, I hope you’ll make a contribution before our
upcoming end-of-month fundraising deadline.

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1015?c0=q32025_abs_em.yMOq2I&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1007&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]EXPRESS DONATE $10

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1016?c0=q32025_abs_em.Buknaj&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1008&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]EXPRESS DONATE $25

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1017?c0=q32025_abs_em.uHcj1U&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1009&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]EXPRESS DONATE $50

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1018?c0=q32025_abs_em.JaOjw6&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1010&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]EXPRESS DONATE $100

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1019?c0=q32025_abs_em.9BFHaA&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1011&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]EXPRESS DONATE $250

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q32025_abs_em.sQXYU5&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1012&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]ANOTHER AMOUNT

Thanks for stepping up.

— Adam

[ https://act.adamschiff.com/go/5307?refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_EOM_AC90_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=1013&akid=8819%2E5257348%2EOvI366 ]Adam Schiff for Senate Banner

135 E. Olive Ave., #750 Burbank CA, 91502

name: Team Bohannan
subject: Christina asked us to talk about the cuts to Iowa PBS
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112069180/559316459/-975735282?refcode=em_logoheader&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNzAzZmZhYy1jYzY4LWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=uWyvdL0Kd-QXv4Me1UmsB0fLEo36bvsXDXSjgUNOE_E=)
_QC News:_ **Iowa PBS’ budget slashed by $3.5 million by Trump’s federal cuts**
Peter,

Sometimes, it’s hard to see how decisions made in Washington affect our communities here in Iowa. Party elites and corporate interests are counting on that confusion. They want to make their cuts quietly, hoping we won’t notice the consequences until it’s too late. **_Christina isn’t letting that happen._**

She asked us to share exactly how the latest budget vote — backed by Mariannette Miller-Meeks — is going to hit home.

**Just last week, Miller-Meeks and her allies in Congress voted to slash $9 billion from the foreign aid and public broadcasting budgets.**_Iowa PBS_ is set to lose $3.5 million in funding — the single largest cut to the station in its 55 years. _Iowa Public Radio_ could lose over $1 million.

_Let’s put that in perspective._

_Iowa PBS_ reaches all 99 counties. It broadcasts trusted national programming, award-winning local shows, and, for the first time this year, every Iowa high school sports championship, giving our student-athletes the spotlight they deserve.

_Iowa Public Radio_ and _PBS_ also support vital community outreach in education and civic engagement. In many rural areas, public broadcasting is a lifeline to the rest of the country. It's often the only reliable source of local news, weather alerts, educational programming, and public safety information. Cutting these funds will be disastrous and leave our neighbors disconnected.

Christina knows how much our communities rely on these services. Mariannette Miller-Meeks may think she can quietly gut public broadcasting without anyone noticing, but we’re paying attention.

clean_body: | = QC News: Iowa PBS’ budget slashed by $3.5 million by Trump’s federal cuts Peter, Sometimes, it’s hard to see how decisions made in Washington affect our communities here in Iowa. Party elites and corporate interests are counting on that confusion. They want to make their cuts quietly, hoping we won’t notice the consequences until it’s too late. Christina isn’t letting that happen. She asked us to share exactly how the latest budget vote — backed by Mariannette Miller-Meeks — is going to hit home. Just last week, Miller-Meeks and her allies in Congress voted to slash $9 billion from the foreign aid and public broadcasting budgets.Iowa PBS is set to lose $3.5 million in funding — the single largest cut to the station in its 55 years. Iowa Public Radio could lose over $1 million. Let’s put that in perspective. Iowa PBS reaches all 99 counties. It broadcasts trusted national programming, award-winning local shows, and, for the first time this year, every Iowa high school sports championship, giving our student-athletes the spotlight they deserve. Iowa Public Radio and PBS also support vital community outreach in education and civic engagement. In many rural areas, public broadcasting is a lifeline to the rest of the country. It's often the only reliable source of local news, weather alerts, educational programming, and public safety information. Cutting these funds will be disastrous and leave our neighbors disconnected. Christina knows how much our communities rely on these services. Mariannette Miller-Meeks may think she can quietly gut public broadcasting without anyone noticing, but we’re paying attention. Thank you, Team Bohannan Christina Bohannan is a mom, law professor, former engineer and state representative. She's running for Congress to defeat Mariannette Miller-Meeks and flip IA-01 blue. Chip in to support her campaign today >> | = = | PAID FOR BY CHRISTINA BOHANNAN FOR CONGRESS P.O. name: Dr. Raul Ruiz
subject: I’m reintroducing the Mobile Cancer Screening Act
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE
body:
While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/dAfRehWDrkq5GdVTndjWgA2?unsubscribedata=4WILVJJSyFX2ueJzeVcvv4pL6Wsl5zsHDnNPSNevVpFqsCxeYvQ3P5WumImBu4jTe4bDjSW%2Bc9RC%2FgYH9fN%2B6ulkpBpB2XeEWwqhTC4F%2F%2BZx0QP9%2FJO7S0AAvMDOtv46dCK3FPveExhpn%2FSf%2BH8vynkfiAhYyYGGX5PM4IM%2FbMHPTyg53KlM15FdKY72bJxxViLU2SXK8QQO5tzlkFNeleQK8Vz48uCPdAzumiLeJFCvglZP3I%2B1Io8O%2BFgZ0mhpEX813ilPIA8%2FtBcLKPNH%2FblmpEW4%2BBSKEgYFO2o3Y6o1tgKwBGyX%2BZrIYPDrVPXcIYUEbX1S%2FgVKLAOzseiWcjZq41k%2BmiIzBYAWi3pdiuwYxovlZUXu9NsjKu1U6l%2BpUC3J3KH3WkZWYSv1HbMTUUw%2B00c%2FLLohRAGN0hP4KQf4MzR6wdeWzmB0jwwVpsEf)
---
| |
---
Last month, Republicans voted to gut healthcare for millions of Americans — and I can tell you, the damage will hit rural and underserved communities the hardest, including right here in California’s 25th District. That’s why I’m reintroducing the Mobile Cancer Screening Act — a bill to bring life-saving cancer screenings directly to the people who need them most. While Republicans are ripping away healthcare access, **I’m fighting to expand it.** I’ve seen what happens when patients can’t get to a clinic. I’ve seen lives lost because screenings came too late — or didn’t come at all. The Mobile Cancer Screening Act will help change that by deploying mobile screening units to rural communities that have been left behind for far too long. I believe that if you run for office, you should be doing it to help people, not hurt them. But too many in Congress have forgotten that. Next year’s election is our chance to hold Republicans accountable. CA-25 is one of the most competitive districts in the country — and we can flip the House blue if we win here. The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. **Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.**

clean_body: While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Last month, Republicans voted to gut healthcare for millions of Americans — and I can tell you, the damage will hit rural and underserved communities the hardest, including right here in California’s 25th District. That’s why I’m reintroducing the Mobile Cancer Screening Act — a bill to bring life-saving cancer screenings directly to the people who need them most. While Republicans are ripping away healthcare access, I’m fighting to expand it. I’ve seen what happens when patients can’t get to a clinic. I’ve seen lives lost because screenings came too late — or didn’t come at all. The Mobile Cancer Screening Act will help change that by deploying mobile screening units to rural communities that have been left behind for far too long. I believe that if you run for office, you should be doing it to help people, not hurt them. But too many in Congress have forgotten that. Next year’s election is our chance to hold Republicans accountable. CA-25 is one of the most competitive districts in the country — and we can flip the House blue if we win here. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = Thank you for your support, Raul | | | ---|---|--- = The son of hardworking farmworkers, Dr. Ruiz grew up in Coachella, CA and learned at an early age that the key to attaining the American Dream is hard work and a great education. In the summer of 1990, Raul walked in the desert heat from business to business in the Coachella Valley asking them to invest in their community by contributing to his education. With each investment for college, he made a promise to come back home and serve his community as a physician. In 2012, he decided to take his service to the community to the next level by running for Congress. Dr. Ruiz needs our help to continue fighting for us in Congress.** | = = PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: You can also =. Dr. Raul Ruiz for Congress Committee P.O. Box 1566 Indio, CA 92202 United States

body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj206ph5wra1vndoizardhjumtqdtwylox0xkprvs4djjd4qimy5oevzk85f0duzfccrh58rajfqinzu2py5v&__stmp=szx86h&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1r7n3ecn3lvoet1lth9dmnzim191r717qucyojw4ugfz7aatg6ihcjj0ul4lbx58zww0fum3yz79mvaphf57p067b9w7mtycttmhula2ed5u9vcwkkil2bj3h9j30vo8ffu1ng7mxe&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol1r7n3ecn3lvoet1lth9dmnzim191r717qucyojw4ugfz7aatg6ihcjj0ul4lbx58zww0fum3yz79mvaphf57p067b9w7mtycttmhula2ed5u9vcwkkil2bj3h9j30vo8ffu1ng7mxe&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=szx86h&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: Worse now than ever before
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter —
This message is longer than what I usually send. I hope you’ll read through to the end.
I started my career as an investigative journalist and led a team that exposed corruption and war crimes all over the world. I have studied and exposed political corruption. And the harsh reality is that America has one of the most corrupt political systems in the Western world.
Trump, with his crypto scams and family deals, has taken it to a new, shocking, unprecedented level.
But corruption in American politics isn’t new. In fact, it’s a big part of why we got Trump in the first place.
Sometimes when people think about corruption they think about acts that are themselves illegal. Gold bars buried in politicians’ yards. Bags of cash exchanged at meetings in hotel rooms.
**Perhaps the most insidious thing about political corruption in our system is that much of it is _legal,_ and therefore less obvious... but no less destructive.**
**Ever since _Citizens United_ opened the floodgates, our system has run on corporate money, secret money, billionaire money... and it’s worse now than ever before.**
_This_ is why public policy doesn’t work for ordinary people. _This_ is why the United States Congress would defund nursing homes to pay for tax cuts for the rich.
Because when members of Congress aren’t begging for money from lobbyists, they’re trying to dodge getting carpet-bombed by Super PACs. Senators get threatened with millions and millions of dollars of attack ads over the votes we take.
And that corruption is why Americans pay a fortune for prescriptions. It’s why insurance claims for a child’s necessary care keeps getting denied. It’s why the United States Congress so often works on behalf of the elite and the powerful while throwing ordinary people to the wolves.
**The people’s elected representatives don’t represent the people, they represent the big donors.**
This is a big part of why we got Trump.
He promised to attack a broken system. It’s a ripe target.
Yes, the system really is rigged. But here’s the thing: **_Trump’s not unrigging it. He’s re-rigging it for himself.**_
He has set free the mob of criminals who violently ransacked the Capitol on his behalf.
He’s purged independent watchdogs across the government who investigate corruption and abuse.
He’s stacked the most powerful offices, including sensitive positions in the military and intelligence community with unqualified personal loyalists.
He’s called on those same loyalists to investigate and prosecute his opponents, while leaning on them to cover up the release of the Epstein files.
He and his family are using the office of the Presidency to enrich themselves in a way we have never, _ever_ seen in American history. They’re selling gold sneakers, gold phones, crypto meme-coins... whatever they can to make a buck from those looking to trade money for influence.
It’s the grip of money on our politics that led us to this moment of crisis. And so even when this fight to stop Donald Trump is won — _and we will win it_ — the broader fight against corruption will define America’s future.
***
When I first ran for Congress, it was because I saw that the election of someone so unfit for the Presidency was a danger to the country. And you got behind me because you saw the same thing.
When we won the Senate it was because we still understood together the urgency and necessity of the moment, and we knew Georgia could lead the way.
**Now, the nation is once again turning to Georgia in a moment of crisis. Can I count on you to fight alongside me, Peter?**
**[If you share my outrage at this unprecedented corruption and abuse of power — and if you are determined to WIN — please chip in to my campaign today. This is the most important Senate race in the country, and I can’t win without you.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250724-1-f&t=1&akid=576%2E5691347%2ElAvU-1)
Thank you,
Sen. Jon Ossoff
**[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250724-1-f&t=2&akid=576%2E5691347%2ElAvU-1)**

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/signup/fewer-emails-signup/?t=3&akid=576%2E5691347%2ElAvU-1) to sign up to receive fewer emails, or click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=576%2E5691347%2ElAvU-1) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

name: David Hogg
subject: I want to talk about my shoes
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I wear the same shoes every day.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250724-1&t=1&akid=2427%2E587540%2EaGihbh)
Peter, I want to talk about my shoes, which I know sounds weird, but hear me out.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250724_1_davidshoes?refcode=em20250724-2427&amount=5&refcode2=2427_587540_aGihbh&akid=2427%2E587540%2EaGihbh)
I wear the same pair every day, and honestly, they're getting seriously beat up.
I've worn them door-knocking in the snow in some unpronounceable Wisconsin town for Susan Crawford. I've worn them knocking doors in Tallahassee, Florida. And most recently, they hit the pavement in Arizona for Deja Foxx.
**Here's what makes Leaders We Deserve different:** we don't just write checks and disappear. When we endorse a candidate, I show up. I knock doors. I make sure our funding is being put to the most effective use possible. And we help candidates run the best campaign possible.
That's because this work is personal. Every door I knock, every conversation I have with a voter, every mile these shoes walk — it's all possible because of you.
Your donations don't just fund campaigns from a distance. They put me on planes to pivotal races. They get me on the ground in swing districts. They enable us to provide hands-on support to young candidates who need more than just money — they need someone who believes in them enough to show up.
These shoes have walked through a lot of states, and they're going to walk through a lot more. But only if we have the resources to keep this grassroots movement going.
**[Can you chip in $5 today to keep us on the ground in the races that matter most? Every dollar gets us one step closer to electing the leaders we deserve.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250724_1_davidshoes?refcode=em20250724-2427&amount=5&refcode2=2427_587540_aGihbh&akid=2427%2E587540%2EaGihbh)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250724_1_davidshoes?amount=5&refcode=em20250724-2427&refcode2=2427_587540_aGihbh&akid=2427%2E587540%2EaGihbh)**
Thank you,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250724-1&t=2&refcode2=2427_587540_aGihbh&akid=2427%2E587540%2EaGihbh)**

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: I’m blown away.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
I’m on a mission to make Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home – and that message is resonating with over 27,000 of you in all 83 of our counties.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=542%2E181023%2E5fLWQO)
Hi team, I’m blown away.
We just closed the books on our fundraising report, and _we broke a record for amount raised in the first six months of a wide open Governor’s race in Michigan!_
That’s because of you, and thousands of people across Michigan who believe in our movement.
I’m on a mission to make Michigan the best place to be a kid, raise a kid, and call home – and that message is resonating with over 27,000 of you in all 83 of our counties.
We all want our loved ones to be happy, healthy, safe, and nearby. We all want to be able to afford to buy a home in the community we want to live in, and then be able to heat that home and drink safe water in that home. We all want a state government that saves you time, saves you money, makes your life easier, and keeps you and your loved ones healthy and safe. A state that is the best place in the country to come home.
That’s the Michigan I’m fighting for, and just like I’ve done in my statewide campaigns before, I’m going to continue working to earn the support of everyone — no matter their party affiliation or zip code — so we can keep moving Michigan forward.
I’m so excited for what’s ahead. Let’s go!
Jocelyn
| [DONATE](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q32025.A5XsW4&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_TYE_AC_20250724_PM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=542%2E181023%2E5fLWQO)

name: VoteVets.org
subject: Hellcats
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
VoteVets has a critical role to play in the fight for the U.S. House of Representatives, and we can’t do that work without your help.
#
Peter:
The original Lady Hell Cats were the Marine Corps Women’s Reserve in World War I. Thousands of women Marines, making the same pay as men of equal rank, at a time when women didn’t yet have the right to vote.
They were revolutionary.
Then, in World War II, Elizabeth Hooker, Barbara Kibbee Jayne, and Cecil Kenyon became the first women test pilots, flying Navy planes. Their first assignment was the F6F Hellcat. Hellcats went on to notch more than 5163 aerial victories, more than half of all Navy and Marine kills in World War II.
Now, in 2025, Hellcats are back in service.
[](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-july25.sScHXv&refcode=em250724-1-120&t=1&akid=34490%2E3013755%2EHHHQ2R)
If we have a majority in the House in 2027 it will be because these Hellcats helped us get there, but we’ve got a lot of work ahead. GOP led states are already trying to stack the deck against us by gerrymandering their congressional districts and suppressing votes.
We have to be ready to fight them with everything we’ve got, and we’ll be supporting the Hellcats every step of the way.
Fundraising, organizing, strategy — you name it, we’re in. And to help make sure the Hellcats have the ammo they need to win, we’re asking you to make a donation.
[**Please, chip in to VoteVets so we can support candidates like Maura Sullivan, Rebecca Bennett, Joanna Mendoza, and Cait Conley. VoteVets has a critical role to play in the fight for the U.S. House of Representatives, and we can’t do that work without your help.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-july25.sScHXv&refcode=em250724-1-120&t=2&akid=34490%2E3013755%2EHHHQ2R)

clean_body: VoteVets has a critical role to play in the fight for the U.S. House of Representatives, and we can’t do that work without your help. Peter: The original Lady Hell Cats were the Marine Corps Women’s Reserve in World War I. Thousands of women Marines, making the same pay as men of equal rank, at a time when women didn’t yet have the right to vote. They were revolutionary. Then, in World War II, Elizabeth Hooker, Barbara Kibbee Jayne, and Cecil Kenyon became the first women test pilots, flying Navy planes. Their first assignment was the F6F Hellcat. Hellcats went on to notch more than 5163 aerial victories, more than half of all Navy and Marine kills in World War II. Now, in 2025, Hellcats are back in service. If we have a majority in the House in 2027 it will be because these Hellcats helped us get there, but we’ve got a lot of work ahead. GOP led states are already trying to stack the deck against us by gerrymandering their congressional districts and suppressing votes. We have to be ready to fight them with everything we’ve got, and we’ll be supporting the Hellcats every step of the way. Fundraising, organizing, strategy — you name it, we’re in. And to help make sure the Hellcats have the ammo they need to win, we’re asking you to make a donation. If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | ** | | | | | | | ** Thank you, The team at VoteVets PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS.ORG, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE’S COMMITTEE Use of military rank, job titles or photographs in uniform does not imply the endorsement of the Department of Defense, Departments of the Army, Navy, Air Force, or the Marines or Space Force.

subject: How do you see the Supreme Court?
body:
Your participation in Hillsdale College’s National Survey on the United States Supreme Court is requested.
| | | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/u/7NQ2fx2mDP0NGckYEHG7yKRxdpQcYCC6M21NbHNXLsN60YRaaJBx3ohdptOvKNTy5luFXuzwH2w0mDOffQ9tsW44spAE5KIISBvpVqjyd5Z7aPFCfuFowC4bYphP6wJpShfkjO5zAF0KOuicbUicCVAA4soycYz0l6OvafRwFfvKVCmnYtVoFExz/NQqtoz0fOx0Fzce76nC6pR9TdpkD2dMA/11)_

| | | Dear Friend, Your participation in Hillsdale College's **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** is requested.

Your response will only take a few minutes, but the subject is crucial to the future of liberty and the separation of powers in America.

The American Founders thought that the proper role of the Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution.

But progressives see the Court differently. They want activist judges to legislate from the bench.
## Do you agree that the role of the Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution as it is written and NOT to make laws or advance a particular policy agenda?
[YES](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/L78haNxgphRuUFccgeLBMU6Kx1ZfB12R_5hCINIRh6zxgJvYV2c1Na0XxxoDiFimeiq96CTp72IdZnOmpfGb5_HWgjSYimlLIpv2MQnbeslruWNMjrUk7l8eWeFa0SocdiAl6HZ_DrnOr2i1tow9PZduNmXGp-yA3wEk4DEkWVDDjJH025IR6RWCppxD4Oa9tjQS8pgMy5V6OytTEBm2h9hV1eInlOVw9NZGLPaXWOCdwH6KYsYuo_fldtbGH3699-OFh-6pSE4o3GqwYEq669hlcAHUZWYFkMtrLq32wTnB2i8o__bDkbLMLIALWG-_i3urwFBoDn575BPLtkgj3jz_9A6mdHFoqtecI4rhQwL_9IBlpWXBadXpzDZY9VK1doKKWMMqSzDb5z-9A0rMdMpMmdFMerHzE609w2Ni4Yc5ZlgBtARs0oBSgYUGgpH3GA23k43PK0W8gYH7wLovbKhbgHWm0czuqJtOpP7zZmqlVYsfSnc6MUeS-8AqRNgt3nXECmD5XA-CgTWU7I_4u7CnHA5V74_LHcWYOO78fB-ePRkHvCJK2QblKsbroaxi8BF6LCJ5PEP_CgiDk0VTS9_nFrwQkv0ynvBkc18-XQuu1TRKDA9hrMJTI8hQE7WqF_TXYnekkKW1Z8mOJ8Um9ncKjX002rrfcevlAgQv8c-5CeBcFgDosK9pW7Ok4Stt8KlwxUorH9h9e0ModAnYu-1hiTp3gtvKEQ/P1I9P6eAa21RtzOhqz-IpVXfXV8UyACW/11) [NO](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/sxg1YMqLJgMGgcaT0FWWtaHjAiOVjw_FVqiWvUt96SHYbcWVqyAfFftxAIb29xmXHZsCg4gS5p2uYtAVZy_uGK64OffxY7Z0vqYA_IjkyZv5RYj53-ydkwoSztgjxA8VdcNFIhcHnHKHEXQG9bwn6kTYd_4RHjIplhGA-A4NzlCxGQ5fJfNWAO4OghMneXEFF_x6VFbeFTE31PAJKyPzkFGhT_J5qQh5BSdw-i3QDpHkVgdcU4z-waAOBS97toIyiFI18YiAg8k_EJkZE4I1vOcA84o-GOgW4Qm9w4NYOW3-8SLf7niqOSdyVe1BwShpr1yZi3mxOhMbiRonYY0FU4Tr5AZnWAfkOR-MJhkpkS_WxN_fxWJJ4IYKi0zyJz1A5zIZ1pH76SbF9l8PKLUIwd8RUNRcOG_NdLE1RyX87NtEKl_vYK739yxUjMYpEHPu51Bb1b06UnX1klBe1Odq_1RxDZd43Bry8qik6yTsPhQkSPcKd0AwllLFTZYkLkSGnktbBqdyT-qWQWu0yyqELZyXEf5qjPnorU3YOF16aYGM3DNFX2G7MJcPRc7UJsgs5stlsFd7elOU5yqQ1hgMYIAIF0pkqWSUHnT5cHbbof55fLSm81f6x4l4Wo36UFzrmrhz-cWX7CniggshZ21BwrPnl3pz-eAhDE4r4b0JZhUB4xQmQzPwos_-PLeqVdoCptUSz5Jl78gH1PIY8A_fi8-OQVlucp_CXQ/m7P0LmRXtj6FQSNxfeDZgsw6F765ZRRB/11) Even worse, progressives dismayed by recent Supreme Court decisions want to "pack" the bench with additional justices to advance a preferred policy agenda.

As Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) put it in a _Boston Globe_ op-ed: "To restore balance and integrity to a broken institution, Congress must expand the Supreme Court by four or more seats."

That is why I'm reaching out to you today...

_What is your opinion about the Supreme Court 's role in our constitutional republic? _

Please share your thoughts with me using the College's **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** right away.

Your response will help Hillsdale form effective strategies to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty--including the proper role of an independent judiciary that applies the Constitution instead of legislating from the bench.

You can easily complete your **National Survey on the United States Supreme Court** using this secure link: **[https://info.hillsdale.edu/supreme-court-survey](http://links.liberty-pulse.com/s/c/vFrLJIxve20M1K5fdzMbhS_Xk0gRVRFhpYxT_AANDoYWaOBNrKZPY1srsE_yEgr7vBfs9HGbMQrDJIc60MM_fPMWClcHJI6uZ4D0SqjMTqcivI7_IrARAI9UyM7h9LGJBhWAIDVcGqAigHsOT4jUASv2UMinIO-qMrJNMb8Oid4uzg1Oj1yTeVW5oo4BRfn-eS5KHYi2mDJWBuIYrdFXdVWPNOTueaxfBtF-C0tlyHRgjDmy93E97lBIWXXgQ9awBozq0bidQus5gy2Id_Msy2jXJtJp6valIxtMXeIwcpNT1umq3vtdCy3LBGhhfUyJuJPvty6UoIfvtNzlumJyOx7JU6xHZ2qJLikhPjFQqb64toAVUOa_E0zTvvrK0N1QlgFDXXfpSPKziSdU1mV9FFoVMK3TTdG97EPScZhOdwLCs_jIKF-WafN-CLp4168udzhq1aU9xu9flUWQbyEyWbAIKgIxFZ8wCcsHr9LrXAkxPLAX8VefQVKe0JtR4I-IifiEpg5pXl8J5ytEIHIkUURV-TB8xotNLDUmR18QMw4DXgQIHvn_Il_KqqtJpVOQri-q6gMikKuWLm_gd0F5cy8-vdTKYi6ftLpMcE7x10TlLgO788rzp2JoDzV_IjLr4gKaCep_uva5G19aE8JsV1pBlLTsNKu4TfrFkjEVKfSjQKhhfU8v3Bb0yKsVhA1P0p8Igac1Xw9gAS3yIk5X15TfBhMnflX3yg/CY1uIXvZzXvOqQa6WgGkMzq5aqSaLn8_/11)** Thank you for your time and attention--and for your support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844

name: Jasmine Smith
subject: Help us build the pipeline
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge America
body:
Emerge alum and current Secretary of State Jena Griswold is running for Attorney General in Colorado. And as she steps up to higher office, another Emerge alum, State Senator Jessie Danielson, is running to take her place.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112070911/559321840/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-07-24_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY1NzFlMzRiLWQxNjgtZjAxMS04ZGM5LTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NU7SmrBVZstq-pII7kGU91gDQteU2jjRv2rfgh1qAnU=)
---
| Peter,

At Emerge, we believe in a simple but powerful idea: **lift as you climb.**

Now in Colorado, we’re seeing that in action. Emerge alum and current Secretary of State Jena Griswold is running for Attorney General in Colorado. And as she steps up to higher office, another Emerge alum, State Senator Jessie Danielson, is running to take her place.

This is how we build real political power — by supporting women to rise, and helping them lift others as they climb. [_**Your membership fuels this pipeline of leadership. clean_body: Emerge alum and current Secretary of State Jena Griswold is running for Attorney General in Colorado. And as she steps up to higher office, another Emerge alum, State Senator Jessie Danielson, is running to take her place. | = | Peter, At Emerge, we believe in a simple but powerful idea: lift as you climb. Now in Colorado, we’re seeing that in action. Emerge alum and current Secretary of State Jena Griswold is running for Attorney General in Colorado. And as she steps up to higher office, another Emerge alum, State Senator Jessie Danielson, is running to take her place. This is how we build real political power — by supporting women to rise, and helping them lift others as they climb. = | = Gratefully, Jasmine Smith Deputy Development Director, Emerge PS. When women lift as they climb, we all rise. = * | | = Paid for by Emerge America Emerge America 818 Connecticut Avenue NW, Suite 450 Washington, DC 20006 United States | = =

name: JAG
subject: Will NYC Be the Next Soviet State?
body:
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=d90de9325e56408ead72c25107e032f8&_e=UW_0pV8doXmIoay19OYNy8G0YckdzlunKR1PlpfA2P9CW7Nc7ZuIT3PM6G56pKDtbGVv0Fn0gvJ1uuzTyZn9WSA1tDM-4JTLVfc4blOThodIpf-3ELDKw8l3TZQlF7sqsPe2OkZ5ZoBcJQobih168BCvKXpBu9ZJSCNsEOJLQDvQEfaooG4BxIsjsw8XqEWicnKqI8RJ8zrc7_fydFsinHflo94GWFFWC_x2VkMFq8_-Tgs-7qga-7ZQykluTf-wb-u1ap6PfDRVC8tDdwT2wRYvrmn881ZeJeoIViTAgZQPTLcsKMhoKhGxdz6YfrIlB319AA5qckoBujf9DYrmXiqabrSRFjtX5zOjt2YdzCJEsbr42Kb_GUraZ_EmAjIoU8fTkYyXPrpEF4lj5YiVfFQhJrnKertqCyjzJCCot8JLY4kkq5LajBn714L79qw4Ma6aQiWHGM94zrue741qIHzNbKw5ssDF2azNkm1H5XG2wWKZO6tEYfpAn3xgIdhtLm-2idbBI48VLmwIEelpWy_HqmjzHaPYYQrKHo7X8ddWrhC7biwu2wJcyChSp0QmI2xHBb4OJdfDQqaOkZ9Z5ajeDpe7t_ZdlFCxGnFeFj2jQwHfyO6WEx3svikvY6Hi)
Peter,

Zohran Mamdani -- an open socialist running for mayor of New York City -- is proposing the very policies which Ayn Rand's villains gleefully imposed.

He is pushing for government seizure of property, racial reparations, rent cancellation, and wealth redistribution on a massive scale.

Mamdani doesn't believe in _earned_ success. He believes in _assigned_ victimhood.

He doesn't believe in empowering individuals. He wants to punish achievement and use race and class as weapons to divide.

Mamdani's ideas lead to breadlines, not breakthroughs.

When I first read Ayn Rand's _We the Living_ , which drew directly from her own experience growing up under Soviet communism, I was haunted by the images of breadlines... of lives wasted waiting on the state... of the erosion of personal freedom... of dreams strangled by envy, fear, and force.

**Today, I see those same destructive ideas rearing their ugly heads in America 's greatest city.**

The fact that Mamdani could win a primary and now has a chance at leading what was once our nation's bastion of capitalism demonstrates that these are more than just bad ideas. They are signs of a greater problem: the growing popularity of the victim mentality.

Unfortunately, Mamdani is not alone. His brand of envy-fueled politics is gaining traction with young people across the country.

That's why the work we do at The Atlas Society is more important than ever before.

I'm Jennifer Anju Grossman, CEO of The Atlas Society. Through our viral videos, graphic novels, online courses, and live events, we are reaching the next generation and giving them the tools to reject the ideology of victimhood and pathological altruism.

**Your gift of $25, $50, or even $100 will help us break the chains of Mamdani 's toxic ideology and give the next generation the tools to live freely, think independently, and achieve greatness.**

Rand called art _" the indispensable medium to convey moral ideals."_

That's why the Atlas Society leverages art to reach new audiences with our action-packed animated book trailers, provocative Draw My Life videos, and captivating graphic novels.

For many young people, this is the first time they will learn about Ayn Rand's work.

By introducing the next generation to the empowering principles of Objectivism, we provide a rational and moral alternative to the culture of collectivism and envy.

Instead of teaching _victimhood_ , we teach **individual agency**.
Instead of promising _handouts_ , we promote **hard work**.
Instead of _scapegoating_ the successful, we **celebrate** them.

But we can't do it without your help.

clean_body: Peter, Zohran Mamdani -- an open socialist running for mayor of New York City -- is proposing the very policies which Ayn Rand's villains gleefully imposed. He is pushing for government seizure of property, racial reparations, rent cancellation, and wealth redistribution on a massive scale. Mamdani doesn't believe in earned success. He believes in assigned victimhood. He doesn't believe in empowering individuals. He wants to punish achievement and use race and class as weapons to divide. Mamdani's ideas lead to breadlines, not breakthroughs. When I first read Ayn Rand's We the Living , which drew directly from her own experience growing up under Soviet communism, I was haunted by the images of breadlines... of lives wasted waiting on the state... of the erosion of personal freedom... of dreams strangled by envy, fear, and force. Today, I see those same destructive ideas rearing their ugly heads in America 's greatest city. The fact that Mamdani could win a primary and now has a chance at leading what was once our nation's bastion of capitalism demonstrates that these are more than just bad ideas. They are signs of a greater problem: the growing popularity of the victim mentality. Unfortunately, Mamdani is not alone. His brand of envy-fueled politics is gaining traction with young people across the country. That's why the work we do at The Atlas Society is more important than ever before. I'm Jennifer Anju Grossman, CEO of The Atlas Society. Through our viral videos, graphic novels, online courses, and live events, we are reaching the next generation and giving them the tools to reject the ideology of victimhood and pathological altruism. Your gift of $25, $50, or even $100 will help us break the chains of Mamdani 's toxic ideology and give the next generation the tools to live freely, think independently, and achieve greatness. Rand called art " the indispensable medium to convey moral ideals." That's why the Atlas Society leverages art to reach new audiences with our action-packed animated book trailers, provocative Draw My Life videos, and captivating graphic novels. For many young people, this is the first time they will learn about Ayn Rand's work. By introducing the next generation to the empowering principles of Objectivism, we provide a rational and moral alternative to the culture of collectivism and envy. Instead of teaching victimhood , we teach individual agency. Instead of promising handouts , we promote hard work. Instead of scapegoating the successful, we celebrate them. But we can't do it without your help. If you believe, as I do, that America needs a revival of the values that made it great, I'm asking you to . Thank you for helping us fight back. Jennifer Anju Grossman (JAG) CEO, The Atlas Society P.S. Mamdani is reviving the same soul-crushing ideas that turned millions of people into state dependents and crushed the best among them. Young people do not understand how a collectivist state grinds down the human spirit, erodes personal freedom, and destroys individual potential, making fear, envy, and ideological conformity a daily reality. The Atlas Society wants to stop that from happening here. The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39920%2E3422442%2EEOSN3y)
Dear Peter,
Yesterday, Speaker Mike Johnson shut down the House until September to avoid a vote on releasing the Epstein files. After months—even years—of MAGA activists pushing for “transparency,” House Republicans now want to be silent.
**The House GOP is making it 100% clear: Congress is there to suck up to Donald Trump, not to deliver for the American people.**
**With Congressmembers failing to hold Trump accountable, our state governments are the last line of defense.**
That’s why Katie is running for governor. She is going to fight for what families need, not what the wealthy and well connected want.
She’s only ever been motivated by one thing: making Californians’ lives better. That’s what she did for us in Congress, and that’s what she’ll do as governor.

name: Jose Javier Rodriguez
subject: The corruption behind Alligator Alcatraz
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSE JAVIER RODRIGUEZ FOR FLORIDA ATTORNEY GENERAL
body:
This is exactly why I'm running for Attorney General. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112071181/559322797/1546157161?refcode=em_JJR_POLL_072425&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTE3Y2FkZDUtZDE2OC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qPo1dz2BEm4RJkb9_yyQl8aKKPVnBAWbR8Oe6NBlYxw=&emci=2f815ede-b868-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=e17cadd5-d168-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=32374386)
---
**_Miami Herald: Contractors building Alligator Alcatraz have donated money to Florida GOP, DeSantis_** You know what they say, Peter — follow the money to find the truth. It turns out that three of the contractors profiting from the cruel Alligator Alcatraz detention center in the Everglades **bankrolled campaigns for Governor DeSantis and Florida Republicans.** Together, these three companies have poured millions into GOP organizations, with _CDR Maguire and its affiliated company, CDR Health,_ alone contributing $1.9 million, and Gothams LLC’s founder adding $50,000. But this isn't the first time these donors have been rewarded for their political loyalty. These same companies have received _billions_ in taxpayer-funded state contracts over the years. This is exactly the kind of corrupt pay-to-play politics that's been destroying Florida. Political donors get rich off taxpayer-funded projects while working families struggle with skyrocketing costs. | | That's exactly why I'm running for Attorney General — to break up this rigged system. **But I want to hear from you: Are you tired of corrupt politicians rigging the system for their wealthy donors?**
---
| [YES](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112071182/559322856/-2073429987?refcode=em_JJR_POLL_072425&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTE3Y2FkZDUtZDE2OC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qPo1dz2BEm4RJkb9_yyQl8aKKPVnBAWbR8Oe6NBlYxw=&emci=2f815ede-b868-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=e17cadd5-d168-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=32374386) | [NO](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112071183/559322862/-2073429987?refcode=em_JJR_POLL_072425&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDkxMjYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTE3Y2FkZDUtZDE2OC1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=qPo1dz2BEm4RJkb9_yyQl8aKKPVnBAWbR8Oe6NBlYxw=&emci=2f815ede-b868-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=e17cadd5-d168-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=32374386)

### The $7.25 federal poverty wage is literally killing Americans, and it must change
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jul 24
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169173828&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MTczODI4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MzM5MTM3NSwiZXhwIjoxNzU1OTgzMzc1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.e98ISvKKne6uumGKAcLDWT6DU-4pPbJ_BNHb_3dOiPw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

---

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=169173828&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY5MTczODI4LCJpYXQiOjE3NTMzOTEzNzUsImV4cCI6MTc1NTk4MzM3NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.7xGRf9J35kvzy9t434ClI0MLgTYVZpwBtRCR_jUunFs)
Sixteen years ago today--July 24, 2009--Congress raised the federal minimum wage to $7.25 an hour. Since then, corporate profits have skyrocketed, executive pay has exploded, and the cost of living has soared.
But the minimum wage? Still stuck at $7.25.
Let me be clear: this isn't just bad policy. It's economic violence. And it's long past time we called it what it is--and demanded better. Here are six truths every American should know about our corrupt wage system. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..yKMq_pyJwqjQtHIETuIZYkzF_nZBTpANEm6q8Z7MR1Y?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=169173828)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5b43654c-0fda-4b50-b5f5-de53523f0774?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **1\. If Wages Kept Pace with Profits, You 'd Earn $30/Hour**
That's not a typo. If the minimum wage had grown at the same rate as corporate profits since 1979, it would be close to $30/hour today. That's about $60,000/year--what we call a _livable_ wage.
So when we fight for $15/hour, we're not demanding justice. We're making a compromise. And corporations refusing to pay even that--while posting record profits and handing out billion-dollar bonuses--are engaged in systemic wage theft.
### **2\. No, It 's Not Just Teenagers Working for Minimum Wage**
Let's kill that tired myth right now. The average minimum wage worker is 36 years old. Over 89% are 20 or older. More than half are women. A quarter are raising children. And 57% work full-time.
This isn't about teenagers earning pocket change. This is about adults supporting families--about people working 40, 50, 60 hours a week and still not earning enough to survive. Those who claim these are "temporary jobs for teens" are either deliberately lying or painfully ignorant. Either way, do not listen to them.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a89f1f7d-12f7-47cb-a218-88b5995f5b8e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### **3\. The Minimum Wage Was Meant to Be a Living Wage**
When President Franklin D. Roosevelt signed the National Industrial Recovery Act in 1933, he didn't mince words:
> "No business which depends for existence on paying less than living wages to its workers has any right to continue in this country."
The minimum wage was designed to ensure dignity--not desperation. Today's stagnant wage violates the very spirit of that promise. Corporations like Walmart who pay their workers so low that they are forced to rely on social welfare are in fact exploiting Americans. Walmart, according to FDR, does not have the right to continue to do business in this country. And if we had politicians with spines, instead of funding from corrupt corporations, we could ensure Walmart, Amazon, Target, and more pay an actual living wage--not an exploitative poverty wage.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..yKMq_pyJwqjQtHIETuIZYkzF_nZBTpANEm6q8Z7MR1Y?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=169173828)
### **4\. Congress Knows Better --They Just Don't Care**
Let's be real. Congress understands the power of inflation. That's why they raised _their own campaign contribution limits_ from $1,800 to $3,500. They made sure the wealthy could give more to their campaigns--but left working Americans earning the same $7.25 an hour.
The cruelty isn't accidental. It's policy.
### **5\. $79 Trillion in Stolen Wages**
A RAND Corporation study found that over the past 40 years, [$79 trillion in income has been redistributed from the bottom 90% of Americans to the top 1%](https://substack.com/redirect/2ffe871d-1b95-4b34-8d5e-e3437e1ca7e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Yes, that's Trillion, with a T. So whenever you hear the super wealthy talk about the "evils" of redistribution of wealth," just know that their ill gotten gains have come from redistributing our wealth to the top 1%.
That's a flood of wealth--stolen from the people who built this country and funneled to the people who least need it. It has made us less safe, less secure, less healthy, and more impoverished.
### **6\. Poverty Is a Killer**
Three in five Americans are living paycheck to paycheck. And poverty doesn't just hurt--it kills. Studies show that for every $1 increase in a person's wage, suicide rates drop by 1%. That's not abstract policy. That's life and death. The National Institute of Health [reports](https://substack.com/redirect/31a1b040-f916-4aa2-9a83-a67e30dac8c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> A recent study funded by NIMHD found that every $1 increase in the minimum wage of US states could reduce the suicide rate in people with high school education or less by 6%. The researchers used National Vital Statistics System data on suicides in people aged 18-64 years from all 50 states and the District of Columbia for every month from 1990 to 2015. The data included race, age, and education level of the people who died by suicide.
In this exploitative backdrop, it is clear that poverty is not simply a personal failure and it is certainly not a life choice--so we must stop allowing policies that make it like a life sentence.
### **In Conclusion: It 's Time to Raise the Wage**
There is no excuse. Not after 16 years. Not when corporate profits are hitting record highs, CEOs are raking in millions, and working people are barely scraping by. This isn't an economic oversight. It's a moral failure.
The bottom line is this. Raising the wage isn't radical, its morally sound. What's radical is forcing full-time workers to live in poverty while billionaires hoard trillions. What's radical is letting poverty kill while pretending we can't afford to do better. What's radical is calling this justice.
It's not enough to be outraged--we have to be organized. We have to fight back. And we have to keep telling the truth, because too many people in power are counting on our silence. So please continue to speak loudly. Let politicians know that by taking corporate money, they lose your support. Demand not just a raise to $15/hour, but to a living wage that commensurates with the immense profitability created by the working class. That's what true economic justice looks like. That's what we must continue to fight for.
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**

clean_body: The $7.25 federal poverty wage is literally killing Americans, and it must change | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription! The $7.25 federal poverty wage is literally killing Americans, and it must change | | | Jul 24 | | | | | | | | | | | | Sixteen years ago today--July 24, 2009--Congress raised the federal minimum wage to $7.25 an hour. Since then, corporate profits have skyrocketed, executive pay has exploded, and the cost of living has soared. But the minimum wage? Still stuck at $7.25. Let me be clear: this isn't just bad policy. It's economic violence. And it's long past time we called it what it is--and demanded better. Here are six truths every American should know about our corrupt wage system. Let 's Address This. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- 1\. If Wages Kept Pace with Profits, You 'd Earn $30/Hour That's not a typo. If the minimum wage had grown at the same rate as corporate profits since 1979, it would be close to $30/hour today. That's about $60,000/year--what we call a livable wage. So when we fight for $15/hour, we're not demanding justice. We're making a compromise. And corporations refusing to pay even that--while posting record profits and handing out billion-dollar bonuses--are engaged in systemic wage theft. 2\. No, It 's Not Just Teenagers Working for Minimum Wage Let's kill that tired myth right now. The average minimum wage worker is 36 years old. Over 89% are 20 or older. More than half are women. A quarter are raising children. And 57% work full-time. This isn't about teenagers earning pocket change. This is about adults supporting families--about people working 40, 50, 60 hours a week and still not earning enough to survive. Those who claim these are "temporary jobs for teens" are either deliberately lying or painfully ignorant. Either way, do not listen to them. | | ---|---|--- 3\. The Minimum Wage Was Meant to Be a Living Wage When President Franklin D. Roosevelt signed the National Industrial Recovery Act in 1933, he didn't mince words: "No business which depends for existence on paying less than living wages to its workers has any right to continue in this country." The minimum wage was designed to ensure dignity--not desperation. Today's stagnant wage violates the very spirit of that promise. Corporations like Walmart who pay their workers so low that they are forced to rely on social welfare are in fact exploiting Americans. Walmart, according to FDR, does not have the right to continue to do business in this country. And if we had politicians with spines, instead of funding from corrupt corporations, we could ensure Walmart, Amazon, Target, and more pay an actual living wage--not an exploitative poverty wage. 4\. Congress Knows Better --They Just Don't Care Let's be real. Congress understands the power of inflation. That's why they raised their own campaign contribution limits from $1,800 to $3,500. They made sure the wealthy could give more to their campaigns--but left working Americans earning the same $7.25 an hour. The cruelty isn't accidental. It's policy. 5\. $79 Trillion in Stolen Wages A RAND Corporation study found that over the past 40 years, . Yes, that's Trillion, with a T. So whenever you hear the super wealthy talk about the "evils" of redistribution of wealth," just know that their ill gotten gains have come from redistributing our wealth to the top 1%. That's a flood of wealth--stolen from the people who built this country and funneled to the people who least need it. It has made us less safe, less secure, less healthy, and more impoverished. 6\. Poverty Is a Killer Three in five Americans are living paycheck to paycheck. And poverty doesn't just hurt--it kills. Studies show that for every $1 increase in a person's wage, suicide rates drop by 1%. That's not abstract policy. That's life and death. The National Institute of Health : A recent study funded by NIMHD found that every $1 increase in the minimum wage of US states could reduce the suicide rate in people with high school education or less by 6%. The researchers used National Vital Statistics System data on suicides in people aged 18-64 years from all 50 states and the District of Columbia for every month from 1990 to 2015. The data included race, age, and education level of the people who died by suicide. In this exploitative backdrop, it is clear that poverty is not simply a personal failure and it is certainly not a life choice--so we must stop allowing policies that make it like a life sentence. In Conclusion: It 's Time to Raise the Wage There is no excuse. Not after 16 years. Not when corporate profits are hitting record highs, CEOs are raking in millions, and working people are barely scraping by. This isn't an economic oversight. It's a moral failure. The bottom line is this. Raising the wage isn't radical, its morally sound. What's radical is forcing full-time workers to live in poverty while billionaires hoard trillions. What's radical is letting poverty kill while pretending we can't afford to do better. What's radical is calling this justice. It's not enough to be outraged--we have to be organized. We have to fight back. And we have to keep telling the truth, because too many people in power are counting on our silence. So please continue to speak loudly. Let politicians know that by taking corporate money, they lose your support. Demand not just a raise to $15/hour, but to a living wage that commensurates with the immense profitability created by the working class. That's what true economic justice looks like. That's what we must continue to fight for. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Different tactic. Still illegal.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250724&t=1001&akid=1894%2E1088799%2Eamchb4 ]Rob Bonta

Quick update for you on our ongoing lawsuit
against the Trump administration for illegally defunding
AmeriCorps:

Last month, I filed a lawsuit challenging Trump’s attempt to gut
AmeriCorps, our national volunteer public service agency — and we secured
a court order to stop the illegal withholding of tens of millions of
dollars in funding.

Now, Trump is trying a new — but similarly illegal! — tactic to withhold
funding, so we’re going back to court. Today I filed an amended lawsuit
that adds the White House Office of Management and Budget as a defendant
and brings new legal claims in our case.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250724-pet/?t=1002&akid=1894%2E1088799%2Eamchb4 ]As we continue litigation, I’m asking you to sign my petition in
support of AmeriCorps — a program that represents the very best of who we
are and can be as Americans, engaging hundreds of thousands of people in
serving their communities every year.

ADD YOUR NAME

AmeriCorps members selflessly serve in food banks, homeless shelters,
schools, veterans’ facilities, and so much more — helping tackle real
challenges in our communities and address critical needs.

I won’t stop fighting Trump’s unlawful attempts to dismantle AmeriCorps —
for the thousands of volunteers who are eager to serve others, and for the
thousands of communities that are stronger because of it.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/survey/em-250724-pet/?t=1003&akid=1894%2E1088799%2Eamchb4 ]If you support AmeriCorps, please join me today by adding your name.

Thanks for speaking up with me.

-Rob

[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/1001?t=1004&akid=1894%2E1088799%2Eamchb4 ][IMG]

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

1700 Tribute Road, Suite 201
Sacramento, CA 95815

subject: I understand.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Crane for Congress
body:
I get it - you’re tired of politicians...
I understand, Patriot.
I know you're tired of politicians emailing you asking for money, and I don't blame you. With inflation still being reeled in, every dollar counts.
But let me tell you, **this is different.**
My name is Eli Crane, and I'm a pro-Trump Navy SEAL Conservative warrior from Arizona. I've been on the front lines, fighting for our country in 5 war-time deployments.
I'm not afraid to take a stand: _I've always proudly championed Trump's America First agenda, I'm for a secure border, I'm for election integrity, and I'm for protecting our kids from woke indoctrination._
Democrats hate me, and the Establishment Republicans aren't too fond of me either. That's why they're throwing everything they've got at me - **a whopping $50 MILLION war chest** to take me down in 2026.
But I'm not backing down. I'm a Navy SEAL, and we don't give up. **_[That's why I'm asking you, with all humility, to chip in a few bucks before my critical fundraising deadline.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=77ddb586f5f248a390a61853c45d1f65&_e=tebsyCCbP_wMocPImhFiEaME_3ejebZiSB3lyIvFaE5vBQlEzvtTGyk8vLrv4m1jARmDEFVG4pR4Pj2Rz1P3slKmyca7S2ReC70pbgzuoi9my77qXbm_ch6u644rq-ZGFGJW-Buwq_OJhdJ_BeYSzPoyJB-2S2bi8uHzAtu5p_jBMSO8NSmx9GNahaioCugPB0foEShIvismQ8nR4ms1PvzNt2Xqj69DzqBy8w3NfOM3hq5jN80mT5z8_pZz5mJLpEvkCkqENqLWNYc_Pr-tPbvKifJcTF3qg10tJ2Rt3EJdovr9zn7-ylJ6LwAleufH1_xrm_3jGVVshbmAAtaeK6ZbNWvH14HuuMLEebuZ7n5Ku2pBuap_kDPoiWcIbc_uUd_NmsRDJn4B00-w54Chi1vsBNFSNIQgIwqjvKzhPrzAQV5PjmVnzQWBl5yUDnMXhN5PlIr_0RbYP6yHEk838UnF7U-Inqou6Et-nWQlhM5oMJ9J5I9NSehgOCXmGvo9O6S1OkO6__5x4CV_rhRXQcCRTy1f8Rnvn5-YLNZ9QxEv58r5NjEKsYipJeasiQXzngQR8dwB9Tg2QT0OE4lMZUh5jLG3VSP9ddMVR7Uqjx-Tz-P4Iv0IR5gTsBUq1-bgLOr83qKDJ2gJ4o-5dp7MIxq911EhSYrvUQLdmQCY7a3uFPciv4w7O5pikS6QHWHT8XjuYmjfUmrnDX8h3qZsm9ni_bGLoHmitPj3kxxqUwd_TVFiV0b92Tdvb7ihrmIB48Cycxdh-llxIhsiA_Gs2WnzBAR21mGIFEnUyao_mW8%3D)_**

clean_body: I get it - you’re tired of politicians... I understand, Patriot. I know you're tired of politicians emailing you asking for money, and I don't blame you. With inflation still being reeled in, every dollar counts. But let me tell you, this is different. My name is Eli Crane, and I'm a pro-Trump Navy SEAL Conservative warrior from Arizona. I've been on the front lines, fighting for our country in 5 war-time deployments. I'm not afraid to take a stand: I've always proudly championed Trump's America First agenda, I'm for a secure border, I'm for election integrity, and I'm for protecting our kids from woke indoctrination. Democrats hate me, and the Establishment Republicans aren't too fond of me either. That's why they're throwing everything they've got at me - a whopping $50 MILLION war chest to take me down in 2026. But I'm not backing down. I'm a Navy SEAL, and we don't give up. I know times are still tough, and if you can't afford to give, I understand. But I'm counting on patriots like you to help me take on the Establishment and the radical Left. My candidacy is in your hands. God bless you.\- Eli Crane | | To ensure that you don't miss out on important messages, please add info@email.craneforaz.com to your address book. Not Sure How?
